source.title: Night Haven Lab setup guide - defend all four sites (Rainbow Six Siege)
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions with heavy ASR noise on room/gadget names ('breach' rendered as 'brereech', 'Kaid' as 'zombie'/'zomby' near the rafters keyboard-barrier section which is corrected in context to Kaid's Electroclaw, 'Kiba Barrier' rendered as 'keyboard barrier'/'zombie keeper barrier', 'Capitao' rendered as 'capau'). Generic ranked setups, explicitly not pro-league lineups, recorded ahead of a cosmetic-only map modernization (creator states the layout itself will not change). Room/callout names are the creator's informal in-video callouts, not verified wiki callouts. No labeled, text-annotated official top-down blueprint could be found for this map at rewrite time (Ubisoft's map page does not expose a readable floor plan; the four blueprint images available via third-party sites carry no room-name text, so they could not be used to ground compass directions). Per the parsing spec, no compass directions have been added to this file's locations — they remain room/feature names only.

reinforcements.note: Electrify with Bandit, Kaid, or Mute.
Keep one reinforcement spare. Lets the Server player dive back onto site under pressure.

reinforcements.location: Right-hand side of the Server wall
reinforcements.note: Reinforce.
Reinforce both and electrify. Watch for Ash opening the window to destroy nearby Bandit batteries.

reinforcements.location: Command Center Left-Right Wall, Left Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce. Pair with head holes on the right side of the same wall.

Reinforce the whole triple wall behind the pillar at the back of site; pair with head holes across it. Skip the pro-league left/right-reinforce-plus-middle-rotate version — gets you domed from the door outside unless you're at a high rank.
Reinforce; pair with feet holes. Don't stand close to this reinforcement — hang back or you'll get domed from the door or elsewhere.

headHoles.location: Command Center Left-Right Wall, Right Side
headHoles.note: Keep soft. See the breach and Connector through here.

headHoles.location: Command Center Triple Wall, Head Holes
headHoles.note: Lined up across the reinforced triple wall instead of a middle rotate hole.

rotates.location: Vault rotate near Command Center / Rafters
rotates.note: Only make this if someone is actively playing Rafters (e.g. Azami holding it) — otherwise it hands attackers a free way onto site. Lets the Rafters player dive back in under pressure.
Rotate for flexibility into Connector. If the Connector/Server walls stay closed and attackers push through Electrical instead, use this to cross and pressure that side.

gadgetPlacements.note: Electrify the two reinforced Connector walls with whichever of these you have.
Feet holes catch anyone jumping in through the window above before they see you — see feet, shoot heads.
Stand at a specific spot and lob a C4 over the window to land outside; a prox alarm won't work the same way here — its throw arc is straighter than C4's looping arc.
Electrify this wall too.

gadgetPlacements.location: Rafters, handrail in the middle (not the edge, not the far side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Kiba Barrier on this specific middle/darker section of the handrail lets you peek around both sides of it.

gadgetPlacements.location: Rafters, higher up near the window
gadgetPlacements.note: Second Kiba Barrier placement, higher up, to see feet of anyone jumping in through the window.

operatorRecommendations.note: Whichever you have available should electrify the Connector and Electrical walls.
Needs to be an experienced/confident gunner. Runs the two Kiba Barrier placements on the handrail. Only make the vault rotate near Command Center if Azami (or whoever) is actually holding Rafters.

generalTips.note: Get a drone or two on attackers pushing through the Connector door and apply light pressure — don't die here unless it's a free kill. If the Connector defender dies, attackers can reach Connector in roughly 30-50 seconds.
Once the Connector wall is open, don't stand or peek along the long sightline down to the far cover point — an outside roof angle sees all the way through this line.
Electrical needs a dedicated player holding the jump-in.
Watch for a Maverick torching a line across the bottom of the Electrical/Server wall from the steps outside, giving attackers a Rafters-area angle.
The door near Server needs a prox alarm or similar. Expect to get peeked from the nearby corner if you sit there too long.
This top floor site and Connector specifically are the most important areas of the top floor to hold.
Fish Stairs (also called Blue/Water Stairs) needs a prox alarm, Kapkan mine, or similar — attackers who walk in unnoticed here get a free pick on defenders holding the Command Center head holes.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce all three basement hatches (Cargo, Animus, Exo/Exo Suit).
One of the three reinforced hatches.
One of the three reinforced hatches.
Reinforce.
Two reinforcements on this wall, watching the default plant spot.

Reinforce. Preferred over reinforcing the right wall — defenders on the left can still cross to reinforce/hold the right wall, whereas defenders on the right get cut off from Animus by the Exo Suit stairs head holes.
Reinforce.

headHoles.note: 100% head holes, not reinforced. Holds the bottom of the Exo Suit stairs.

headHoles.location: Single wall near Nano, top-right corner
headHoles.note: Covers the default plant spot reached by tucking in from Animus.
See the top of the Cargo stairs; pair with feet holes for anyone walking down the stairs from Exo, and to see feet when attackers drop the nearby hatch.

headHoles.location: Hole near Animus breach (window jump spot)
headHoles.note: Made in the floor for the window-jump angle. Place tight to the wall, away from the window, so attackers can't see the hole before jumping in.

hatches.note: Reinforced. No vert directly above the Animus breach, making Bandit-tricking this area relatively safe.

gadgetPlacements.location: Left of the Exo hatch, hole in the wall
gadgetPlacements.note: Roughly 40% of the time attackers jump through the nearby window here — hold below with a shotgun for a near-free kill. Make the hole tight to the wall so attackers can't spot it before jumping.
Same technique, but most attackers throw a Ram/breaching drone through here instead of jumping. Make it tight to the wall for the same reasons.
See anyone walking in through the door; also watch the side of the tank. Always make these.
Hold this position and also cover the soft ceiling above it, or you'll get pushed out entirely.
Place a Proximity Alarm or goo mine (e.g. Kapkan/Ela-style trap) for audio warning the moment attackers push onto the default plant.

gadgetPlacements.location: Hole to counter the Animus vert-and-hatch attack
gadgetPlacements.note: If attackers take the vert above Animus and drop the hatch to plant, throw a C4 or smoke through this hole to disrupt the plant in the corridor below.
Hold upstairs above the basement — this is where all the vert for Exo, Assembly, and Animus comes from.

operatorRecommendations.note: Actively waste attacker time upstairs to stop them freely opening vert onto Exo, Assembly, and Animus.
Bandit-tricking is very feasible here since there's no vert directly above the breach.

generalTips.note: Watch for: take the Animus vert, open the hatch (no breach needed), come through, and plant with smokes covering both doors. The only counter is the dedicated C4/smoke hole into the corridor.
All of the ceiling area above Exo/Assembly/Animus is soft floor — a coordinated stack that takes this vert can deny defenders from standing almost anywhere below.

reinforcements.location: Two walls near the main Lobby breach, Reinforced Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce; pair with head holes on one and a rotate on the other.
Reinforce at the start of the round from the stairs position outside site. No confirmed counter, though a Capitao bolt has reportedly been found that can stop it.
Reinforce to hold Games/Lounge. Two reinforcements are left spare if you'd rather not use them here.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Connector (upstairs mirror of the top floor Connector)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced, same as the top-floor Connector walls.

headHoles.location: Wall facing the main Lobby breach
headHoles.note: Throw C4s and smokes through here onto the breach to delay pushes.

headHoles.location: Two walls near the main Lobby breach, Head Hole Side
headHoles.note: Head holes for C4s toward the door and the breach, and for smoking the breach out.

rotates.location: Small rotate near the stairs outside site
rotates.note: Lets the player holding the stairs outside come back onto site.

rotates.location: Two walls near the main Lobby breach, Rotate Side
rotates.note: Paired with the reinforcement on the other side of the same wall area.

rotates.location: Upstairs, same rotate as top-floor Connector
rotates.note: Reused rotate structure from the top floor Connector setup.

gadgetPlacements.note: Feet holes all the way along; do not reinforce this wall.
Start here holding the stairs; watch the Kitchen window and call out drops. Fall back through toward the breach once pressured if the hatch is still closed.
Barricade both doors and play the room.
Feet holes covering both windows for anyone jumping in; also watch the bottom of the stairs (can vault over but not shoot over the gap).

operatorRecommendations.note: Barricades both doors into Games/Lounge; peek toward the corridor and breach direction from here once set up.

generalTips.note: Third most common site seen in ranked play, though Control/Storage (site 4) is the stronger/easier site to defend if your team is coordinated enough.

Attackers get easy, early control of the area around the main Lobby breach since the only thing on the far side of it is a Lobby door — expect early pressure into the Kitchen corridor and Kitchen itself.
Once the vert is made upstairs (all the floor above Kitchen/Meeting is soft), defenders get pushed further back.
Common attacker plant pattern once the breach is open: smoke off one specific angle, then dive up and plant near the breach — counter with the C4/smoke head holes.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce.
Reinforce a single wall out into Lounge/Games. Default choice; only skip this in favor of head holes if committing to holding Lounge/Games instead (see generalTips).
Reinforce both.
Reinforce both, same pattern as the top-floor Command Center setup.
Reinforce, mirroring the top-floor Command Center triple wall treatment.

reinforcements.location: Upstairs wall toward Fish Stairs, right-hand side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce.

headHoles.note: Head holes toward the breach, same as the top-floor Command Center setup.

rotates.note: Rotate here, not on the left — the left side of this wall can be held/watched from a window way outside, which makes the rotate unusable.
Optional: if committing to holding Lounge/Games, add a rotate back into this room since there's otherwise only one door (through Storage) back in if Storage is lost.

hatches.note: Opened (not reinforced) purely as an escape route.

gadgetPlacements.location: Rotate window near back of Control
gadgetPlacements.note: Watch this window — commonly held by attackers, which is why the rotate goes on the right instead of the left.
Barricade this door in addition to reinforcing the external walls.
Hold upstairs actively — this whole area is soft floor above Control, so an unheld upstairs lets attackers jump in and plant almost freely.

operatorRecommendations.note: Option if committing to holding Lounge/Games rather than leaving the connecting wall alone.

generalTips.note: Considered the best/strongest site on the map, comparable to Open Area on Bank or Bar on Chalet/Consulate-style good-but-coordination-dependent sites — requires full 5-stack coordination to defend well.
Upstairs here is defended almost identically to the top-floor Command Center/Connector site — same wall and head hole pattern reused.
Watch for attackers sledging/bucking out the single Animus-facing wall downstairs and walking up the stairs to flank into this site.

If committing to holding Lounge/Games instead of leaving it alone: open the Storage/Lounge wall and make head holes (or a rotate) there instead of reinforcing it, then defend from Storage and peek through as needed.
